ground_truth.title: 10 Best Car Rentals In USA In 2025 [For all Budgets!]
ground_truth.author: Cazzy Magennis
ground_truth.main_content: Whether you're embarking on a full cross-country road trip or just exploring one of the many large cities, having your own vehicle is essential for making the most out of your time in the United States of America! We spent more than 3 months driving the Western states of the USA (+ Alaska) and had an epic time. The freedom of your own car or camper is unparalleled. If you aren't opting to rent a motorhome in the US, hiring a car is your next best option to ensure you have the freedom to get out onto the open road and plan your own itinerary. With over 4000 car rental agencies operating in the US, finding the best car rental company can seem like a daunting task. We always start our search for a car rental on Discover Cars! Discover Cars is an awesome website that allows you to enter your chosen dates and pick-up location, and see heaps of cars that are available to you from tons of trusted rental companies. The article covers 10 best car rental companies in the USA: 1. Budget - Our top pick for affordable prices, easy booking options, and exceptional customer service with locations in every state. 2. Payless - For affordable car rentals with over 80 locations across the country and a strong reputation for excellent customer service. 3. Hertz - The best company for loyalty/rewards program with Hertz Gold Plus Rewards offering perks and discounts. 4. Sixt - For a wide variety of vehicles including SUVs, electric cars, convertibles, sports cars, vans, pick-up trucks. 5. Enterprise - Best for airport car hire with presence in every state of the US and 9000 locations globally. 6. National - For a premium car rental experience with the Emerald Club loyalty program. 7. Thrifty - For car hire for all budgets with 300 locations across the country. 8. Dollar - For budget car rentals with competitive rates and clear fees. 9. Alamo - For family-friendly car rentals with free additional driver for all renters. 10. Avis - For great deals with heaps of discounts and promotions. Common FAQs covered include: costs average $30-$100 per day; minimum age is 21 (sometimes 19 with extra fees); popular road trips include Pacific Coast Highway, Route 66, and Blue Ridge Parkway; hiring a car is worth it for freedom and flexibility to explore national parks.
with: On average, you can expect to pay anywhere from around $30 to $100 dollars per day or more for larger or more premium vehicles.
Renting a car in the US typically requires you to be at least 21 years old, though some companies may permit those over 19.
Some of the most popular road trips include the Pacific Coast Highway, Route 66, and the Blue Ridge Parkway, each offering iconic landmarks and unbeatable landscapes.
When it comes to car rental companies in the US, Budget is our top pick! They offer affordable prices, easy booking options, and exceptional customer service that can't be beaten.
Hiring your own vehicle will give you the freedom to get around the country on your own schedule and allow you to get off the beaten track and into some of the country's incredible national parks.
